title: Verify Tipper Jog Axis Selection And Movement Direction On The HMI
summary: Use the documented Manual Mode control mapping on the operator station HMI jogging screen to identify which axis control affects the left or right tipper and to interpret the expected movement direction for the plus and minus buttons.
when_to_use: Use when reviewing or verifying tipper jog control labeling and expected motion direction on the operator station HMI, including guided troubleshooting, evidence gathering, or interpretation of the Visu_Dual_MCP jogging interface.
not_for: Do not use this runbook as authority to drive an axis into a hard stop.
Do not use this runbook for unsupported maintenance or recovery actions beyond identifying axis mapping and expected jog direction.

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: Interpret the HMI jog control mapping, verify expected plus/minus direction behavior against the manual, and document any mismatch.
Provide access to the operator station HMI or jogging screen view when needed.

safety_notes: If you push the axis until it hits a hard stop, the axis will register a fault.
Stop the check if movement reaches a hard stop.
Use the documented mapping as a reference before or during any guided movement verification.
access_or_tools_needed: Operator station HMI or documented jogging screen view

access_required: Access to the operator station HMI or a captured view of the jogging screen
system_preconditions: The jogging interface associated with the Visu_Dual_MCP screen must be available for viewing or reference.
external_references: OptiSweep Operation and Maintenance Manual, section 5.6 Jogging the Tipper

visual_references.description: Operator station HMI jogging screen showing Manual Mode axis controls for Z1, A1, Z2, and A2.
Operator station control panel reference showing JOG and related HMI controls.
Operator station HMI view showing Manual Mode axis controls in a related operator station context.
Operator station HMI screen associated with Visu_Dual_MCP access during maintenance procedures.
Additional operator station HMI artifact associated with manual axis positioning from Visu_Dual_MCP.

steps.title: Open or view the jogging interface
steps.purpose: Access the HMI view that contains the documented tipper jog controls.
steps.instruction: Open or view the operator station HMI jogging interface associated with the Visu_Dual_MCP screen.
steps.expected_result: The jogging interface is visible for review.
steps.healthy_condition: The screen shows the Manual Mode jogging controls used for tipper axis selection and movement.
steps.failure_condition: The jogging interface cannot be accessed or the expected jog controls are not visible.

actions.label: View jogging screen
actions.exact_action: Open or view the operator station HMI jogging interface associated with the Visu_Dual_MCP screen.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Manual Mode jogging screen with the available axis controls.
Operator station control panel reference showing access to JOG from the main operator screen.
Additional Visu_Dual_MCP-related operator station screen context.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the expected jogging interface associated with Visu_Dual_MCP cannot be identified.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: On the operator station HMI, navigate to the "Visu_Dual_MCP" screen (F3)... Press JOG to open the jogging screen.

steps.title: Identify the axis control under review
steps.purpose: Determine which documented axis label is being checked on the HMI.
steps.instruction: Identify whether the control to be checked is Z1 AXIS, A1 AXIS, Z2 AXIS, or A2 AXIS.
steps.expected_result: The selected HMI control is matched to one of the four documented axis labels.
steps.healthy_condition: The control label is clearly identifiable as Z1 AXIS, A1 AXIS, Z2 AXIS, or A2 AXIS.
steps.failure_condition: The control label is missing, ambiguous, or does not match the documented axis names.

actions.label: Identify axis label
actions.exact_action: Identify whether the control to be checked is Z1 AXIS, A1 AXIS, Z2 AXIS, or A2 AXIS.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Axis labels Z1 AXIS, A1 AXIS, Z2 AXIS, and A2 AXIS on the jogging screen.
Related Manual Mode axis labels on the operator station HMI.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the observed control labeling does not match the documented axis names.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The Z1 AXIS (vertical movement) and A1 AXIS (rotate the gripper) buttons move the left tipper; the Z2 AXIS and A2 AXIS buttons move the right tipper.

steps.title: Match the axis to the documented tipper function
steps.purpose: Interpret which tipper and motion type the selected axis controls.
steps.instruction: Compare the selected control to the documented mapping: Z1 AXIS is left tipper vertical movement, A1 AXIS is left tipper gripper rotation, Z2 AXIS is right tipper vertical movement, and A2 AXIS is right tipper movement.
steps.expected_result: The selected axis is mapped to the correct tipper side and motion type.
steps.healthy_condition: The selected control aligns with the documented left/right tipper and Z/A function mapping.
steps.failure_condition: The observed control labeling or implied function does not match the documented mapping.

actions.label: Compare axis mapping
actions.exact_action: Compare the selected control to the documented mapping: Z1 AXIS is left tipper vertical movement, A1 AXIS is left tipper gripper rotation, Z2 AXIS is right tipper vertical movement, and A2 AXIS is right tipper movement.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Which axis buttons correspond to left tipper versus right tipper on the jogging screen.
Related jog mode reference noting A1/Z1 for the left axis and A2/Z2 for the right tipper.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the observed control labeling or behavior does not match the documented mapping.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The Z1 AXIS (vertical movement) and A1 AXIS (rotate the gripper) buttons move the left tipper; the Z2 AXIS and A2 AXIS buttons move the right tipper.

steps.title: Verify plus direction behavior
steps.purpose: Confirm the documented meaning of the plus jog command for the selected axis.
steps.instruction: Check the movement command direction mapping: + should move the Z-axis up and rotate the A-axis towards the operator.
steps.expected_result: The plus command is interpreted according to the documented direction mapping.
steps.healthy_condition: Plus corresponds to Z-axis up and A-axis rotation towards the operator.
steps.failure_condition: Observed or interpreted plus direction does not match the documented mapping.

actions.label: Check plus direction mapping
actions.exact_action: Check the movement command direction mapping: + should move the Z-axis up and rotate the A-axis towards the operator.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Plus control on the jogging screen and the selected axis context.
Related plus/minus controls shown with Manual Mode axis controls.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the observed movement behavior does not match the documented plus direction mapping.
Stop if movement reaches a hard stop.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The + button moves the Z-axis up and rotates the A-axis towards the operator.
If you push the axis until it hits a hard stop, the axis will register a fault.

steps.title: Verify minus direction behavior
steps.purpose: Confirm the documented meaning of the minus jog command for the selected axis.
steps.instruction: Check the movement command direction mapping: – should move the Z-axis down and rotate the A-axis away from the operator.
steps.expected_result: The minus command is interpreted according to the documented direction mapping.
steps.healthy_condition: Minus corresponds to Z-axis down and A-axis rotation away from the operator.
steps.failure_condition: Observed or interpreted minus direction does not match the documented mapping.

actions.label: Check minus direction mapping
actions.exact_action: Check the movement command direction mapping: – should move the Z-axis down and rotate the A-axis away from the operator.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Minus control on the jogging screen and the selected axis context.
Related plus/minus controls shown with Manual Mode axis controls.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the observed movement behavior does not match the documented minus direction mapping.
Stop if movement reaches a hard stop.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The – button moves the Z-axis down and rotates the A-axis away from the operator.
If you push the axis until it hits a hard stop, the axis will register a fault.

steps.title: Record any mismatch
steps.purpose: Capture discrepancies between the HMI and the documented reference for follow-up.
steps.instruction: Record any mismatch between the observed control labeling or behavior and the documented mapping.
steps.expected_result: Any discrepancy is documented for escalation or review.
steps.healthy_condition: No mismatch is found, or any mismatch is clearly recorded.
steps.failure_condition: A mismatch is observed but not documented, or the discrepancy cannot be clearly described.

actions.label: Document mismatch
actions.exact_action: Record any mismatch between the observed control labeling or behavior and the documented mapping.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Use the jogging screen as the visual reference when documenting any mismatch.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the observed control labeling or movement behavior does not match the documented mapping.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The Z1 AXIS (vertical movement) and A1 AXIS (rotate the gripper) buttons move the left tipper; the Z2 AXIS and A2 AXIS buttons move the right tipper.
The + button moves the Z-axis up and rotates the A-axis towards the operator. The – button moves the Z-axis down and rotates the A-axis away from the operator.

steps.title: Avoid driving the axis into a hard stop
steps.purpose: Prevent creating an axis fault during any movement behavior check.
steps.instruction: Verify that the axis is not driven into a hard stop while checking movement behavior.
steps.expected_result: The check is completed without causing a hard stop fault.
steps.healthy_condition: The axis does not reach a hard stop during the check.
steps.failure_condition: The axis reaches a hard stop and registers a fault.

actions.label: Monitor for hard stop risk
actions.exact_action: Verify that the axis is not driven into a hard stop while checking movement behavior.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Jogging screen context while monitoring axis movement behavior.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop if movement reaches a hard stop, because the axis will register a fault.
Escalate if a hard stop fault is registered during the check.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: If you push the axis until it hits a hard stop, the axis will register a fault.

success_criteria: The user can identify whether the selected control is Z1 AXIS, A1 AXIS, Z2 AXIS, or A2 AXIS.
The user can determine which tipper and motion type the selected axis controls using the documented mapping.
The user can interpret the expected plus and minus movement directions from the documented reference.
No hard stop fault is caused during the check.
Any mismatch between observed labeling or behavior and the documented mapping is recorded for escalation.
healthy_conditions: Z1 AXIS and A1 AXIS correspond to the left tipper.
Z2 AXIS and A2 AXIS correspond to the right tipper.
Plus corresponds to Z-axis up and A-axis rotation towards the operator.
Minus corresponds to Z-axis down and A-axis rotation away from the operator.
The axis does not hit a hard stop during the check.
failure_conditions: Observed control labeling does not match Z1 AXIS, A1 AXIS, Z2 AXIS, or A2 AXIS as documented.
Observed control behavior does not match the documented left/right tipper mapping.
Observed plus or minus direction behavior does not match the documented direction mapping.
The axis is pushed into a hard stop and registers a fault.
The jogging interface associated with Visu_Dual_MCP cannot be accessed or identified.
escalation_guidance: Escalate if the observed control labeling or movement behavior does not match the documented mapping.
Stop and escalate if movement reaches a hard stop or an axis fault is registered.
Escalate if the expected jogging interface associated with Visu_Dual_MCP cannot be identified from the available HMI view.

The + button moves the Z-axis up and rotates the A-axis towards the operator. The – button moves the Z-axis down and rotates the A-axis away from the operator.
If you push the axis until it hits a hard stop, the axis will register a fault.
